name: Profile_R5_AuditEvent_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.AuditEvent resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 AuditEvent resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 AuditEvent resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.AuditEvent resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 AuditEvent resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 AuditEvent resources.
R5: `AuditEvent.category` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (text)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.category` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `AuditEvent.category` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.category` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.category` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: text.

element.short: R5: emergency | alert | critical | error | warning | notice | informational | debug (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.severity` has a context of AuditEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.severity` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
ATNA will map this to the SYSLOG PRI element.

element.short: R5: Workflow authorization within which this event occurred (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.basedOn` has a context of AuditEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.basedOn` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The patient is the subject of the data used/created/updated/deleted during the activity (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.patient` has a context of AuditEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.patient` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Encounter within which this event occurred or which the event is tightly associated (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.encounter` has a context of AuditEvent based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.encounter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.category` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (text)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.category` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `AuditEvent.category` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.category` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.category` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: text.

element.short: R5: Specific type of event additional types
element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.code` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (text)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.code` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.subtype` as `RelatedTo`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.code` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.code` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: text.

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.occurred[x]` additional types (dateTime) additional types from child elements (value)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.occurred[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.period` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.occurred[x]` do not cover the following types: dateTime.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.occurred[x]` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
The time or period can be a little arbitrary; where possible, the time should correspond to human assessment of the activity time.

element.short: R5: Whether the event succeeded or failed (new)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has a context of AuditEvent.outcome based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.code` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
In some cases a "success" may be partial, for example, an incomplete or interrupted transfer of a radiological study. For the purpose of establishing accountability, these distinctions are not relevant.

Element `AuditEvent.outcome.detail` has a context of AuditEvent.outcome based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `AuditEvent`.
Element `AuditEvent.outcome.detail` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
A human readable description of the error issue SHOULD be placed in details.text.

Element `AuditEvent.agent` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.agent` as `Equivalent`.
Several agents may be associated (i.e. have some responsibility for an activity) with an event or activity.

For example, an activity may be initiated by one user for other users or involve more than one user. However, only one user may be the initiator/requestor for the activity. 

When a network are used in an event being recorded, there should be distinct agent elements for the known actors using the network. The agent with a network detail would be the responsible agent for use of that network.

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.source.site` additional types (Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Location)) additional types from child elements (identifier)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.source.site` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.source.site` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.source.site` do not cover the following types: Reference.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.source.site` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: identifier.

Element `AuditEvent.source.observer` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.source.observer` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `AuditEvent.source.observer` with unmapped reference targets: CareTeam.

element.comment:

The mappings for `AuditEvent.source.type` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.source.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: text.

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.entity.securityLabel` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (text)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.entity.securityLabel` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.entity.securityLabel` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.entity.securityLabel` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.entity.securityLabel` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: text.
Copied from entity meta security tags.

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.entity.detail.value[x]` additional types (Quantity, CodeableConcept, boolean, integer, Range, Ratio, time, dateTime, Period)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.entity.detail.value[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.entity.detail.value[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.entity.detail.value[x]` do not cover the following types: boolean, CodeableConcept, dateTime, integer, Period, Quantity, Range, Ratio, time.
The target context `AuditEvent.entity.detail.value[x]` is a choice-type element and cannot directly hold extensions. The context is moved up to parent element `AuditEvent.entity.detail`.

element.definition: R5: `AuditEvent.entity.detail.type` additional types (CodeableConcept) additional types from child elements (coding)
element.comment:
Element `AuditEvent.entity.detail.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `AuditEvent.entity.detail.type` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.entity.detail.type` do not cover the following types: CodeableConcept.
The mappings for `AuditEvent.entity.detail.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: coding.
